pr.title: Check for progress regressions in CI
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the GitHub Actions build workflow so pull requests are checked for decomp progress regressions. The build matrix was simplified from ad-hoc options to explicit modes, with link mode building the PR, saving its progress.json, switching to the repository default branch, rebuilding, and comparing DOL code/data progress with jq. CI now emits GitHub error annotations and fails if either code or data progress decreases. The workflow also adjusted upload conditions to use the new matrix.mode and added sparse checkout settings to a later checkout step.
what_changed: Changed the workflow trigger from a verbose push/pull_request block with push path filtering to `on: [push, pull_request]`.
In link mode, configure.py is run with `--map --require-protos` in addition to compiler, error, verbose, and version arguments.

In link mode, CI now runs ninja on the PR branch, copies `build/${{matrix.version}}/progress.json` to an actual-progress file, fetches and switches to the default branch, runs ninja again, and copies default-branch progress to expected-progress.
Added a jq comparison that requires PR progress `.dol.code` and `.dol.data` to be greater than or equal to default-branch progress.
Added CI error messages `Code progress went down!` and `Data progress went down!`, and exits nonzero when either regression is detected.
In diff mode, CI now explicitly runs `ninja all_source` followed by `ninja diff`.
Updated progress upload and map artifact upload conditions from checks against the old empty matrix option to `matrix.mode == 'link'`.
Added sparse checkout configuration for a later `actions/checkout@v4` step, checking out only `src`, `tools`, `config`, and `.nix`.
smart_moves: Used the generated `progress.json` as the regression source of truth instead of adding a separate progress calculation path.
Compared against the repository default branch inside CI, making the regression test relative to current master rather than a hardcoded threshold.
Allowed equality as passing via `actual >= expected`, so neutral PRs do not fail while regressions are caught.
Separated link and diff behavior with an explicit `matrix.mode`, making the workflow easier to read than overloading empty strings and command fragments.
Used GitHub Actions `::error::` annotations so regressions surface clearly in CI logs.
Kept progress upload and map artifact upload tied only to link builds, preserving prior artifact behavior under the new matrix naming.
Used `set -eou pipefail` in the shell block to make script failures less likely to be silently ignored.
decomp_lessons: Progress regressions are decompilation regressions and can be guarded automatically by comparing `progress.json` against the default branch.
CI checks should distinguish matching/link progress from diff-only validation; explicit modes make this distinction reusable.
When adding regression checks, compare both code and data progress because either can move backward independently.
Default-branch comparison is preferable to static expected percentages in an active decomp project where progress changes frequently.
Build workflow changes can protect review quality even when no game code changes are involved.
naming_conventions: Used mode values `link` and `diff` to describe the intended build behavior.
Used local variable names `config_args`, `src`, `expected`, `actual`, `test`, and `status` in the workflow shell script.
Saved branch comparison files as `actual-progress.json` for the PR build and `expected-progress.json` for the default-branch build.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level or function-matching tactics were changed in this PR.
The relevant matching safeguard is CI-level: fail a PR if generated DOL code or data progress decreases relative to the default branch.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided context.
searchable_terms: Check for progress regressions in CI
follow_up_queries: Find later workflow changes touching progress regression checks in .github/workflows/build.yml.
Search for updates to progress.json schema or `.dol.code` and `.dol.data` fields.
Look for PRs that adjusted matrix.mode link/diff behavior or CI build matrix structure.
Search for follow-up fixes related to git switch default branch during pull_request CI.
